leadText: Unnerve (Japanese: きんちょうかん Tension) is an Ability introduced in Generation V.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation V 1.1.2 Generation VII 1.1.3 Generation VIII onward 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation V [ edit | edit source ] Unnerve causes opposing Pokémon to be unable to eat their own held Berries . However, it does not prevent usage of Natural Gift . When an Unnerve user switches in, the Ability displays a message. If there are entry hazards , then they activate after the message. Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Wild Pokémon are 1.2× as likely to answer calls for help when facing a Pokémon with Unnerve. It shares this effect with Intimidate and Pressure . Generation VIII onward [ edit | edit source ] Unnerve does not prevent opposing Pokémon from eating held Berries when the move Teatime is used. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Unnerve has no effect outside of battle.

sections.title: In battle
sections.text: Contents 1 In battle 1.1 Generation V 1.2 Generation VII 1.3 Generation VIII onward In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation V [ edit | edit source ] Unnerve causes opposing Pokémon to be unable to eat their own held Berries . However, it does not prevent usage of Natural Gift . When an Unnerve user switches in, the Ability displays a message. If there are entry hazards , then they activate after the message. Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Wild Pokémon are 1.2× as likely to answer calls for help when facing a Pokémon with Unnerve. It shares this effect with Intimidate and Pressure . Generation VIII onward [ edit | edit source ] Unnerve does not prevent opposing Pokémon from eating held Berries when the move Teatime is used.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD GtI Foes next to the Pokémon cannot eat or drink. * Foes next to the Pokémon become nervous and can't eat or drink! Thrown items that hit them will still have an effect. * S MD Enemy Pokémon next to the Pokémon become nervous and can't eat or drink! Thrown items that hit them will still have an effect.
